name: librizin-common
title: librizin-common
summary: arch independent files from the Rizin suite
description:
arch independent files from the Rizin suite
Rizin is a fork of the radare2 reverse engineering framework with a
focus on usability, working features and code cleanliness.
.
Rizin is portable and it can be used to analyze binaries, disassemble
code, debug programs, as a forensics tool, as a scriptable command-line
hexadecimal editor able to open disk files, and much more!
.
This package provides the arch independent files from Rizin.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use librizin-common because arch independent files from the Rizin suite.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use librizin-common as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair librizin-common with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `librizin-common --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
